meta.title: Form Collections - Backend Schema
meta.excerpt: Understanding form validation, field types, and dynamic form collection patterns in Strapi.

seo.metaTitle: Form Collections in Strapi | CMS Reference
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to create and manage form collections with validation patterns.

props.text: Form collections in Strapi enable developers to create structured form data with validation rules and field dependencies.

props.body:
Text fields
• Email validation
• Number formatting
• Date pickers
• Rich text editors
• File uploads

props.title: Form Validation
props.body: Always validate input on both client and server sides to ensure data integrity.

props.text: Robust form validation ensures data integrity and provides clear feedback to users. Strapi supports both built-in validators and custom validation logic.

features.title: Required Fields
features.description: Mark fields as required in content-type schema

props.title: Validation Schema Example
Client-Side Validation Only
props.body: Never rely solely on client-side validation. Always validate on the server to prevent malicious submissions.

props.text: Form collection schemas in Strapi define the structure and validation rules for form submissions. Here's a complete example for a contact form collection.

props.title: Complete Form Collection Schema

props.body:
Use appropriate field types (email, enumeration, boolean)
• Set validation constraints (minLength, maxLength, required)
• Include metadata fields (ipAddress, userAgent) for audit trails
• Add status field for workflow management
• Consider GDPR compliance with consent checkbox

props.text: Dynamic forms can be built using Strapi components to create flexible form builders where field configurations are stored in the CMS.

links.title: Form Field Component
links.description: Define reusable field types with validation rules, labels, and placeholders

links.title: Form Layout Component
links.description: Configure multi-step forms, conditional fields, and field grouping

links.title: Submission Handler
links.description: Custom logic for email notifications, CRM integration, and data processing

props.title: Dynamic Form Rendering
Dynamic Form Benefits
props.body: Dynamic forms allow non-technical users to create and modify forms without code changes, enabling rapid iteration and A/B testing.

props.text: Follow these best practices to ensure secure, user-friendly, and GDPR-compliant form implementations.

features.title: Security
features.description: Rate limiting, CSRF protection, input sanitization

features.title: Privacy
features.description: GDPR consent, data retention policies, PII handling

features.title: Performance
features.description: Client-side validation, progressive enhancement, loading states

features.title: Accessibility
features.description: ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, error announcements

props.body:
Implement rate limiting to prevent spam (5 submissions per IP per hour)
• Sanitize all user input before storing in database
• Add honeypot fields for bot detection
• Use HTTPS for all form submissions
• Provide clear error messages with guidance
• Include GDPR consent checkbox with link to privacy policy
• Store IP address and timestamp for audit trails
• Set up automated email notifications for new submissions
• Archive old submissions after defined retention period
• Test forms with screen readers for accessibility

props.title: GDPR Compliance
props.body: Always obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data. Provide users with the ability to request data deletion and export.
